description: §14.1.1 Option D — the F5 closure, end to end. `s.substring(a, b)` is now MODELED as `string` instead of the silent `asIs` escape hatch, so a `match` on that result no longer fires E-TYPE-025 (`cannot match on asIs-typed subject`) and needs NO `const ch: string =` annotation workaround. The modeled type flows through: `classify` matches the first char of "abc" against "a" and the derived cell `firstKind` resolves to "vowel", rendered at #out. Pins BOTH the type-system half (E-TYPE-025 gone) and the runtime effect the tabling enables.
rationale: §14.1.1 — `asIs` is the sanctioned SILENT untyped escape hatch for a value whose shape genuinely cannot be expressed; it is never errored. The Option-D fix (typer-soundness-poison-2026-07-12) NARROWS what falls into `asIs` by modeling the curated invariant-return host-method surface: `string.substring -> string` is a total, invariant fact about the JS runtime scrml emits onto, so encoding it is a sound type rule. With the substring result typed `string`, the §18 `match` subject is a typed primitive (E-TYPE-025 requires a typed subject), so E-TYPE-025 SHALL NOT fire and the match dispatches: the first char of "abc" ("a") selects the "a" arm, so `firstKind` == "vowel".
